findings.evidence: `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/EffectiveBounds/README.md:112` names `index_powMonoidHom_two_le_of_closure`; the new public declaration at line 25 is for all positive `n`.
findings.fix: Make the `n`-power theorem private/internal, or replace it with a direct public square theorem and use any generalization only inside the file.
findings.issue: `index_range_powMonoidHom_le_of_closure_eq_top` publicly exposes an arbitrary positive-power bound, while the roadmap asks only for the squaring-map abstract input.

findings.evidence: `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/EffectiveBounds/Targets.lean:47` states only the `powMonoidHom 2` example; the new public theorem at line 49 quantifies over arbitrary positive `n`.
findings.fix: Make `units_pow_index_le` private/internal and keep `units_sq_index_le` as the public theorem needed by downstream roadmaps.
findings.issue: `units_pow_index_le` publicly exports `[O_F^× : (O_F^×)^n] ≤ n^[F:ℚ]`, which is broader than the Layer-1 unit-square-index target.

summary: The roadmap target is the square-index bound, but the PR publicly exports positive-power generalizations that are not part of the named downstream API. Keep the public surface to the square unit theorem and, if needed, the square abstract group input.
